quiz.title: Unit 4 Quiz 1, Biology I
quiz.subtitle: Cell Biology: Cell Theory & History
quiz.subject: Biology
quiz.topic: Discovery of Cells, Contributors to Cell Theory, Postulates

questions.question: Which scientist first coined the term 'cell' after observing cork under a microscope?
questions.explanation: Robert Hooke observed thin slices of cork in 1665 and described the box-like structures as 'cells' because they reminded him of monastery rooms. He did not observe living cells — only cell walls.

questions.question: Which of these scientists observed the different types of cells in plant tissue?
questions.explanation: Matthias Schleiden, a German botanist, studied plant tissues and concluded that all plants are composed of cells. This was a key contribution to cell theory.

questions.question: Which of these scientists did not contribute to the cell theory?
questions.explanation: Robert Koch contributed to germ theory and microbiology (identifying bacteria that cause tuberculosis and cholera), but not to cell theory. Schleiden, Schwann, and Virchow are the three main contributors.

questions.question: Schleiden contributed to cell theory by stating that:
options: All plant tissues are composed of cells
questions.explanation: Schleiden, a botanist, proposed that all plant tissues are composed of cells. Schwann extended this to animals. Virchow added that cells arise from pre-existing cells.

questions.question: Which scientist demonstrated that all animal tissues are composed of cells?
questions.explanation: Theodor Schwann, a German zoologist, extended Schleiden's work to animals, concluding that all animal tissues are also composed of cells. This unified cell theory across kingdoms.

questions.question: Who first observed living cells like bacteria?
questions.explanation: Leeuwenhoek used superior hand-ground lenses (up to 270×) to observe living 'animalcules' including bacteria, protozoa, and blood cells. Hooke observed only dead cork cells.

questions.question: Who stated 'Omnis cellula e cellula'?
questions.explanation: Virchow stated 'all cells come from cells' in 1855, completing the third principle and founding cellular pathology. This rejected the idea of spontaneous generation.

questions.question: Which of these is not a postulate of the cell theory?
options: Cells are the basic units of life
Living things are composed of one or more cells
questions.explanation: The statement 'all cells contain a nucleus' is NOT a postulate of cell theory because prokaryotic cells lack a nucleus. The three postulates are: (1) all living things are composed of cells, (2) cells are the basic unit of life, and (3) cells arise from pre-existing cells.

questions.question: Matthias Schleiden was a _____
questions.explanation: Matthias Schleiden was a German botanist who studied plant tissues and contributed the first part of cell theory regarding plants.

questions.question: Theodore Schwann was a _____
questions.explanation: Theodor Schwann was a German zoologist who extended cell theory to animals. He also discovered pepsin and studied fermentation.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You have a strong grasp of cell theory history and its contributors.
scoreMessages.70: Good job. Review which scientists contributed which parts of cell theory.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on the three main postulates and who discovered what.
scoreMessages.0: Please review the history of cell discovery and the three principles of cell theory.
